questions.question: What defines a void element in HTML?
choices: An element that is invisible on the page
An element that cannot have any content or closing tag
An element that has been deprecated in HTML5
An element that only contains text, not other elements
questions.explanation:
Void elements are HTML elements that cannot have any child content (text or nested elements) and do not have a closing tag. Examples include
,
, , and .

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a void element?
questions.explanation:
The

element is not a void element; it has both an opening and closing tag and can contain content. Elements like ,
, and are void elements that cannot have content or closing tags.

questions.question: In HTML5, which is the correct way to write a void element?
choices:
Both
and
are valid in HTML5
questions.explanation:
In HTML5, both
and
are valid syntax for void elements. The trailing slash is optional and has no effect. However,
(without the slash) is the more common HTML5 convention, while
is carried over from XHTML.

questions.question: Which void element is used to define the relationship between the current document and an external resource, such as a stylesheet?
questions.explanation: The <link> element is a void element used to define relationships between the document and external resources. Its most common use is linking to CSS stylesheets with <link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css">.

questions.question: The <___> void element is used to insert a thematic break or horizontal rule between sections of content.
questions.explanation: The <hr> element is a void element that represents a thematic break between paragraph-level content. It is typically rendered as a horizontal line and does not require a closing tag.

questions.question: The <___> void element provides metadata about the HTML document, such as character encoding and viewport settings.
questions.explanation: The <meta> element is a void element placed in the <head> section that provides metadata about the document. Common uses include setting the character encoding (<meta charset="UTF-8">) and viewport for responsive design.
